The error `Unexpected token 'P', "PK..." is not valid JSON` occurs because of a violation of React's "Rules of Hooks" combined with attempting to load a binary file (like an FBX) using `useGLTF` (which expects JSON-based GLTF or binary GLB). When conditional logic inside a component changes the order or number of hooks called, React can get confused, leading `useGLTF` to be called on an FBX file, or vice versa, resulting in parsing errors (PK is the header for Zip files, which FBX/GLB files sometimes effectively are, or are treated as binary streams that fail JSON parsing).

To fix this, I have split the model loading into two separate components (`ModelFBX` and `ModelGLTF`) so that hooks are always called unconditionally within their respective components. I also ensured that the file loader components handle auto-scaling of imported models.
